norway.title: Norway, the cold wave keeps rolling
norway.subtitle: Haaland, discipline and ruthless transitions.
paragraphs: Norway is no longer just the team of one giant striker. It has become a football idea: direct, cold, brave and able to punish the smallest mistake.
Against Brazil, Erling Haaland struck twice and turned a prestigious night into a Norwegian statement.
England now awaits: power, rhythm, duels and a football culture both nations understand very well.

england.title: England, the endless meeting with itself
england.subtitle: Talent, pressure and an old dream to turn into a trophy.
paragraphs: England always plays two opponents: the team in front, and the weight of its own story.
The 3–2 win over Mexico showed both the fragility and strength of the Three Lions.
Against Norway, Bellingham and Kane meet Haaland in a quarter-final built for intensity and nerve.

egypt.title: Goodbye Egypt, with respect and regrets
egypt.subtitle: The Pharaohs leave after nearly stunning the world champions.
paragraphs: Egypt leave the World Cup with a heavy heart but their heads held high. They led Argentina 2–0 and came close to a historic upset.
The final minutes will hurt. In thirteen minutes, Argentina turned a match Egypt had prepared almost perfectly. Fatigue, pressure and late-game management proved decisive.
If this was Mohamed Salah’s last major World Cup stage, he leaves as a legend: a leader, a symbol and a player who still carried a nation’s hope.
timeline: End of a dream, respect to the Pharaohs

argentina.title: Argentina turn Egypt around and reach the quarter-finals
argentina.subtitle: Messi misses, creates, scores — and refuses to leave the stage.
paragraphs: The world champions trembled, then struck like great teams do. Trailing 0–2 against a disciplined Egypt, Argentina came back to win 3–2 in a stunning finish.
Romero revived the match from a Messi cross, then Messi equalised with a powerful strike after missing a penalty earlier. In stoppage time, Enzo Fernández completed the comeback.
Argentina survive, Messi reaches eight goals, and Switzerland now await in the quarter-finals.

switzerland.title: Switzerland, the power of calm
switzerland.subtitle: The Nati advance quietly, but with the competitive coldness that troubles favourites.
paragraphs: Switzerland keep moving in their own way: calm, organised and hard to break down. Through on penalties, they now face Argentina.
They may not always dazzle, but they know how to stay alive in matches, close space and wait for pressure to shift.
Against Argentina they will not be favourites, but they have exactly the profile to make a quarter-final uncomfortable.
